text:
First, I stood in a quiet, modern corridor, confronted by glass and reflections and the muffled echo of my own footsteps. Even the emergency equipment—fire extinguisher, exit light—seems to ask for preparedness. I felt the sterile stability of this space, calm yet slightly dislocated, as though I were between destinations.

Then, in the next scene, I faced a vivid wall of ideas: "R+D," glowing in oversized letters, surrounded by narratives of curiosity and reflective inquiry. The screens describe the human relationship to work, place, and self. Here, I felt more engaged—a sense of wonder and belonging, inspired by the invitation to examine my presence as part of ongoing research and discovery. The environment asked me to question, to participate. I felt more alive, more intricately woven into the fabric of collective human enquiry.
